profiles.name: Andrew Huberman
profiles.title: Neuroscientist, Stanford
profiles.bio: Andrew Huberman is a neuroscientist and educator known for making neuroscience, physiology, and behavioral tools useful to a broad audience.

timeline.title: Born in Palo Alto, California
timeline.note: Early life context for a scientist who later focused on visual neuroscience and behavior.

timeline.title: Huberman Lab launched
timeline.note: Began publishing long-form public education on neuroscience, health, and behavior.

profiles.name: Andrew Wilkinson
profiles.title: Entrepreneur, Investor
profiles.bio: Andrew Wilkinson is the co-founder of Tiny, a holding company built around buying, operating, and compounding simple internet businesses.

timeline.title: Founded MetaLab
timeline.note: Built a design agency that became the starting point for a broader company-building path.

timeline.title: Never Enough published
timeline.note: Reflected on ambition, money, status, and the costs of building.

profiles.name: Anne Lamott
profiles.title: Writer
profiles.thesis: Write one honest sentence at a time.
profiles.bio: Anne Lamott is an American novelist and writing teacher whose work is known for its honesty, humor, faith, and practical creative guidance.

timeline.title: Born in San Francisco
timeline.note: Grew up around books and writing.

timeline.title: Bird by Bird published
timeline.note: A durable guide to writing, attention, and creative patience.

profiles.name: Atul Gawande
profiles.title: Surgeon, Writer
profiles.bio: Atul Gawande is a surgeon, writer, and public health thinker who studies how professionals make complex systems safer and more reliable.

timeline.title: Born in Brooklyn
timeline.note: Raised in a medical family before becoming a surgeon and writer.

timeline.title: The Checklist Manifesto published
timeline.note: Turned checklists into a serious tool for high-stakes professional reliability.

profiles.name: Baltasar Gracián
profiles.title: Philosopher, Writer
profiles.bio: Baltasar Gracián was a Spanish Jesuit philosopher and writer whose aphorisms compress practical wisdom about judgment, reputation, and power.

timeline.title: Born in Belmonte, Spain
timeline.note: Entered a world of court politics, religion, rhetoric, and strategy.

timeline.title: The Art of Worldly Wisdom published
timeline.note: Collected maxims on prudence, perception, and conduct.

profiles.name: Cal Newport
profiles.title: Writer, Computer Scientist
profiles.bio: Cal Newport is a computer science professor and author focused on deep work, attention, skill-building, and the discipline required for meaningful output.

timeline.title: Born in Houston
timeline.note: Later became a computer scientist and writer on work systems.

timeline.title: Deep Work published
timeline.note: Popularized focused, high-cognition work as a scarce professional advantage.

profiles.name: Charlie Munger
profiles.title: Investor, Berkshire Hathaway
profiles.bio: Charlie Munger was Warren Buffett's longtime partner at Berkshire Hathaway and a relentless advocate for multidisciplinary thinking, incentives, and avoiding obvious stupidity.

timeline.title: Born in Omaha
timeline.note: Grew up in the same city that shaped Warren Buffett.

timeline.title: Joined Berkshire Hathaway as vice chairman
timeline.note: Helped steer Berkshire toward quality businesses and long-term compounding.

timeline.title: Poor Charlie's Almanack published
timeline.note: Collected talks, mental models, and Munger's way of thinking.

bookTitles: Damn Right!: Behind the Scenes with Berkshire Hathaway Billionaire Charlie Munger
All I Want to Know is Where I'm Going to Die So I'll Never Go There
Seeking Wisdom: From Darwin to Munger

profiles.name: Christopher Nolan
profiles.title: Film Director
profiles.bio: Christopher Nolan is a filmmaker known for large-scale, structurally ambitious movies built around time, memory, obsession, and moral pressure.

timeline.title: Born in London
timeline.note: Began making films at a young age.

timeline.title: Memento released
timeline.note: Established Nolan's interest in structure, memory, and nonlinear storytelling.

profiles.name: David Goggins
profiles.title: Endurance Athlete, Author
profiles.thesis: Expand the standard you answer to.
profiles.bio: David Goggins is an endurance athlete and former Navy SEAL known for his extreme mental toughness and self-discipline philosophy.

timeline.title: Born in Buffalo
timeline.note: His early hardship became central to the story he later told.

timeline.title: Can't Hurt Me published
timeline.note: Made his philosophy of deliberate discomfort widely known.

profiles.name: David Ogilvy
profiles.title: Advertising Executive
profiles.bio: David Ogilvy was a legendary advertising executive who helped define modern direct-response and brand advertising through research, clarity, and craft.

timeline.title: Born in West Horsley, England
timeline.note: Later moved through research, sales, and advertising.

timeline.title: Founded his advertising agency
timeline.note: Built the firm that became Ogilvy & Mather.

timeline.title: Ogilvy on Advertising published
timeline.note: Captured his operating principles for advertising.

profiles.name: Eckhart Tolle
profiles.title: Spiritual Teacher
profiles.bio: Eckhart Tolle is a spiritual teacher and author whose work centers on presence, attention, and loosening identification with thought.

timeline.title: Born in Germany
timeline.note: Later became a spiritual teacher after a major inner shift.

timeline.title: The Power of Now published
timeline.note: Introduced his teaching on presence to a broad readership.

profiles.name: Elon Musk
profiles.title: CEO, Tesla & SpaceX
profiles.bio: Elon Musk is an entrepreneur and engineer associated with Tesla, SpaceX, and other companies pushing aggressive technology and manufacturing frontiers.

timeline.title: Born in Pretoria
timeline.note: Early interest in computers and technology shaped his path.

timeline.title: Founded SpaceX
timeline.note: Started a company around reducing the cost of access to space.

profiles.name: Eric Ries
profiles.title: Entrepreneur, Author
profiles.bio: Eric Ries is an entrepreneur and author best known for the Lean Startup method: rapid experimentation, validated learning, and disciplined product iteration.

timeline.title: Born
timeline.note: Built a career around startups and product learning.

timeline.title: The Lean Startup published
timeline.note: Popularized build-measure-learn and validated learning.

profiles.name: James Clear
profiles.title: Author, Atomic Habits
profiles.bio: James Clear is an author and habits thinker whose work turns behavior change into simple, repeatable systems.

timeline.title: Born in Ohio
timeline.note: Later wrote extensively about habits and personal systems.

timeline.title: Atomic Habits published
timeline.note: Became a widely read framework for habit formation.

profiles.name: John D. Rockefeller
profiles.title: Founder, Standard Oil
profiles.thesis: Systemize the machine, then improve it.
profiles.bio: John D. Rockefeller built Standard Oil into one of history's most powerful companies and became a defining figure in American business, monopoly, and philanthropy.

timeline.title: Born in Richford, New York
timeline.note: A strict, religious, bookkeeping-minded upbringing shaped his habits.

timeline.title: Entered oil refining
timeline.note: Moved into refining as oil became one of the defining industrial opportunities of the era.

timeline.title: Standard Oil founded
timeline.note: Created the company that became the center of his business system.

timeline.title: Standard Oil Trust formed
timeline.note: A new structure helped coordinate control across a sprawling enterprise.

timeline.title: Standard Oil broken up
timeline.note: The Supreme Court ordered the breakup under antitrust law.

timeline.title: Rockefeller Foundation chartered
timeline.note: His later life focused heavily on philanthropy, medicine, education, and public health.

bookTitles: Letters from John D. Rockefeller to His Son
Random Reminiscences of Men and Events
Titan: The Life of John D. Rockefeller, Sr.

profiles.name: Keith J. Cunningham
profiles.title: Entrepreneur, Business Teacher
profiles.bio: Keith J. Cunningham is an entrepreneur and business teacher focused on judgment, financial discipline, and asking better questions before acting.

timeline.title: The Ultimate Blueprint published
timeline.note: A practical business operating manual.

timeline.title: The Road Less Stupid published
timeline.note: Focused on thinking time and better business questions.

bookTitles: The Ultimate Blueprint for an Insanely Successful Business

profiles.name: Lex Fridman
profiles.title: AI Researcher, MIT
profiles.thesis: Long conversations as a learning engine.
profiles.bio: Lex Fridman is an AI researcher and long-form interviewer known for conversations about technology, science, philosophy, and human nature.

timeline.title: Born in Chkalovsk
timeline.note: Later moved into computer science and AI research.

timeline.title: Podcast work expanded
timeline.note: Long-form conversations became his main public learning format.

profiles.name: Morgan Housel
profiles.title: Author, Psychology of Money
profiles.bio: Morgan Housel is a writer and investor known for explaining money, risk, behavior, and long-term decision-making through clear stories.

timeline.title: Born
timeline.note: Built a career as a financial writer.

timeline.title: The Psychology of Money published
timeline.note: Turned behavioral finance lessons into memorable stories.

profiles.name: Naval Ravikant
profiles.title: Entrepreneur, Investor
profiles.bio: Naval Ravikant is an entrepreneur, investor, and thinker known for compact ideas about leverage, judgment, wealth, happiness, and independence.

timeline.title: Born in India
timeline.note: Later immigrated to the United States and built a career in startups.

timeline.title: AngelList founded
timeline.note: Helped reshape early-stage startup fundraising and investing.

timeline.title: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant published
timeline.note: Collected Naval's public ideas on wealth and happiness.

profiles.name: Peter Thiel
profiles.title: Entrepreneur, Investor
profiles.bio: Peter Thiel is an entrepreneur and investor known for PayPal, Palantir, Founders Fund, and contrarian ideas about monopoly and startups.

timeline.title: Born in Frankfurt
timeline.note: Later immigrated to the United States.

timeline.title: Co-founded PayPal
timeline.note: Built one of the defining internet payments companies.

timeline.title: Zero to One published
timeline.note: Turned his startup lectures into a compact theory of monopoly and technology.

profiles.name: Ray Dalio
profiles.title: Founder, Bridgewater
profiles.bio: Ray Dalio founded Bridgewater Associates and is known for principles-based management, macro investing, and systematic decision-making.

timeline.title: Born in Queens
timeline.note: Started investing young and later built a macro investing firm.

timeline.title: Founded Bridgewater Associates
timeline.note: Built one of the world's most influential hedge funds.

timeline.title: Principles published
timeline.note: Presented his approach to life, work, and decision systems.

profiles.name: Richard Feynman
profiles.title: Physicist, Nobel Laureate
profiles.bio: Richard Feynman was a Nobel Prize-winning physicist celebrated for curiosity, clear explanation, playful problem-solving, and scientific honesty.

timeline.title: Born in Queens
timeline.note: Early curiosity and tinkering shaped his scientific style.

timeline.title: Awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics
timeline.note: Recognized for work in quantum electrodynamics.

timeline.title: Surely You're Joking, Mr. Feynman! published
timeline.note: Made his stories and thinking style widely accessible.

profiles.name: Rick Rubin
profiles.title: Music Producer
profiles.thesis: Reduce until the real thing appears.
profiles.bio: Rick Rubin is a music producer known for stripping work down to its essence and helping artists find what feels true.

timeline.title: Born in Long Island
timeline.note: Grew up into a producer with a wide musical range.

timeline.title: Def Jam founded
timeline.note: Helped build one of hip-hop's defining labels.

profiles.name: Rob Fitzpatrick
profiles.title: Entrepreneur, Author
profiles.bio: Rob Fitzpatrick is an entrepreneur and author who teaches practical customer discovery and how to ask questions that reveal reality.

timeline.title: The Mom Test published
timeline.note: A short, practical guide to customer conversations.

profiles.name: Robert Cialdini
profiles.title: Psychologist, Author
profiles.bio: Robert Cialdini is a psychologist whose research on persuasion and social influence shaped how people understand compliance, trust, and decision triggers.

timeline.title: Born in Milwaukee
timeline.note: Built an academic career studying persuasion and influence.

timeline.title: Influence published
timeline.note: Synthesized persuasion research into memorable principles.

profiles.name: Ryan Holiday
profiles.title: Author, The Daily Stoic
profiles.bio: Ryan Holiday is a writer and media strategist who popularized modern Stoicism through books about discipline, resilience, ego, and action.

timeline.title: Born in California
timeline.note: Moved from marketing and media into writing about Stoicism.

timeline.title: The Obstacle Is the Way published
timeline.note: Helped introduce Stoic ideas to a wider business and creative audience.

profiles.name: Shane Parrish
profiles.title: Writer, Mental Models Teacher
profiles.thesis: Make better decisions with better models.
profiles.bio: Shane Parrish is the founder of Farnam Street and a writer focused on mental models, decision-making, and practical wisdom.

timeline.title: Farnam Street started
timeline.note: Began publishing notes on decision-making, reading, and mental models.

timeline.title: The Great Mental Models series began
timeline.note: Turned recurring ideas into a structured series.

bookTitles: The Great Mental Models Volume 1: General Thinking Concepts

profiles.name: Sherlock Holmes
profiles.title: Fictional Detective
profiles.bio: Sherlock Holmes is Arthur Conan Doyle's fictional detective and a durable symbol of observation, inference, and disciplined attention.

timeline.title: First appeared in A Study in Scarlet
timeline.note: Introduced Holmes and Watson as a detective pair.

timeline.title: Short stories began in The Strand Magazine
timeline.note: The format made Holmes a lasting popular figure.

bookTitles: A Few Lessons from Sherlock Holmes

profiles.name: Victor O. Schwab
profiles.title: Copywriter
profiles.bio: Victor O. Schwab was a direct-response copywriter whose advertising work emphasized reader desire, specific promises, and clear selling.

timeline.title: Born
timeline.note: Became one of the classic names in direct-response copywriting.

timeline.title: How to Write a Good Advertisement published
timeline.note: Captured practical copywriting principles that still circulate.

bookTitles: How to Write a Good Advertisement

profiles.name: Warren Buffett
profiles.title: CEO, Berkshire Hathaway
profiles.bio: Warren Buffett is the chairman of Berkshire Hathaway and one of the most influential investors in history, known for patience, temperament, and business quality.

timeline.title: Born in Omaha
timeline.note: Started studying business and investing early.

timeline.title: Took control of Berkshire Hathaway
timeline.note: Turned a struggling textile company into a compounding holding company.

timeline.title: The Snowball published
timeline.note: Alice Schroeder's biography became a major account of Buffett's life.

bookTitles: The Snowball: Warren Buffett and the Business of Life
A Few Lessons for Investors and Managers from Warren Buffett
